name: Banana Cream Pie in Almond Crust
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Crust: Combine butter and sugar in a food processor until smooth. Add flour, almonds, baking powder, and salt; process just until combined. Form dough into a ball, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for 1 hour (or up to 2 days). Flatten the dough into a disk and chill.
Press dough into a 9-inch glass pie plate, trimming off excess so it's even with the edge of the dish. Crimp the edge decoratively. Refrigerate until firm.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line the crust with foil, then fill with dried beans or pie weights. Bake for 12 minutes. Remove weights and foil, then bake until crust is set and edges are golden brown, about 20 minutes. Let cool completely on a rack.
Filling: In a small bowl, sprinkle gelatin over water; let stand 10 minutes to soften. Pour milk into a medium saucepan. Scrape the seeds from the vanilla bean into the milk and add it. Heat until just beginning to simmer. Whisk egg yolks, sugar, and cornstarch in a separate bowl until thick. Gradually whisk hot milk into the yolk mixture. Return this to the saucepan and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture begins to boil and thickens, about 3 minutes.
Remove from heat; stir in softened gelatin. Strain into a bowl and chill until cool, about 30 minutes.
In a large bowl, beat cream and powdered sugar with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form. Gently fold in whipped cream and pastry cream mixture. Stir in bananas until combined.
Spoon filling into the crust. Pipe remaining whipped cream over the top using a pastry bag fitted with a medium star tip. Chill for at least 3 hours, up to 6 hours before serving.
